exercises.title: What does @RestController do?
exercises.expected_solution:
GET /hello → 200 OK, body: Hello, World!
POST /echo → 200 OK, body: HYDERABAD
GET /nothing → 204 No Content, no body

test_cases.expected:
GET /hello → 200 OK, body: Hello, World!
POST /echo → 200 OK, body: HYDERABAD
GET /nothing → 204 No Content, no body

hints: Methods returning a value: 200 OK implicit
ResponseEntity.noContent().build() → 204
@RequestBody String: reads the raw body string

exercises.title: Map HTTP methods to annotations
exercises.scenario: Each HTTP method has its own mapping annotation.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the correct mapping annotation for each method.
hints: GET = read, POST = create, PUT = update, DELETE = remove
CRUD maps to GET/POST/PUT/DELETE

exercises.title: GET endpoint with @PathVariable
exercises.scenario: Extract a URL segment into a method parameter.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @RestController with two endpoints:
1. GET /api/users/{id} → returns 'User: {id}'
2. GET /api/orders/{orderId}/items/{itemId} → returns '{orderId} item {itemId}'

Both return String.
hints: @PathVariable extracts {name} from the URL template
Multiple @PathVariables: name them the same as the URL placeholder

exercises.title: Optional to ResponseEntity — the standard 404 pattern
exercises.scenario: Service returns Optional; controller converts to ResponseEntity.
exercises.instructions:
Write a `ProductController.getById()` method that:
- Calls `service.findById(id)` returning Optional<Product>
- Returns 200 + product if found
- Returns 404 if not found

Use the idiomatic Optional → ResponseEntity pattern.
hints: Optional.map(ResponseEntity::ok) wraps found value in 200 response
.orElse(ResponseEntity.notFound().build()) returns 404 if empty

exercises.title: POST returning 201 Created
exercises.scenario: POST creates a resource — always return 201, not 200.
exercises.instructions:
Write a `@PostMapping` endpoint that:
- Accepts @RequestBody OrderRequest
- Calls service.create(req) returning Order with generated id
- Returns 201 Created with the created Order in body

Include the required ResponseEntity construction.
hints: ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(saved) — 201 + body
@RequestBody deserializes the JSON body to OrderRequest

exercises.title: @RequestParam with defaults and optional
exercises.scenario: Query parameters can be required, optional, or have defaults.
exercises.instructions:
Write a GET endpoint for `/api/products` that accepts:
- `category` (required String)
- `limit` (optional int, default 20)
- `sort` (optional String, no default)

Return a formatted String showing all three values.
hints: required = false: param is optional, null if absent
defaultValue = '20': used if param absent (must be String, parsed to int)

exercises.title: DELETE returning 204 or 404
exercises.scenario: DELETE: 204 if deleted, 404 if not found.
exercises.instructions:
Write a DELETE endpoint for `/api/products/{id}` that:
- Calls service.delete(id) returning boolean (true=deleted, false=not found)
- Returns 204 No Content if deleted
- Returns 404 Not Found if not found

Use ternary or conditional for the ResponseEntity.
hints: ResponseEntity<Void>: no body — void type parameter
ResponseEntity.noContent().build() → 204
Ternary: deleted ? 204 : 404

exercises.title: Complete CRUD controller
exercises.scenario: All five operations in one controller.
exercises.instructions:
Write a complete `StockController` with all 5 CRUD operations:
- GET /api/stocks → all
- GET /api/stocks/{symbol} → one or 404
- POST /api/stocks → 201
- PUT /api/stocks/{symbol} → 200 or 404
- DELETE /api/stocks/{symbol} → 204 or 404

Use a StockRequest record for POST/PUT bodies.
hints: Five methods, five annotations, five status code patterns
POST uses path variable for symbol + body for name+price
GET+PUT use Optional pattern; DELETE uses boolean

exercises.title: Separate request and response records
exercises.scenario: DTO pattern: client sends request record, receives response record.
exercises.instructions:
Write a `UserController.register()` POST endpoint that:
- Accepts `UserRegistrationRequest(String email, String password)`
- Returns `UserRegistrationResponse(String userId, String email, String createdAt)` with 201
- userId is generated (UUID or counter), createdAt is 'now'

Demonstrate the DTO pattern clearly.
hints: Request: email + password (client provides)
Response: userId + email + createdAt (server-set fields included)
Client never sets userId or createdAt — server generates them

exercises.title: Status codes for REST operations
exercises.scenario: Each REST operation has a canonical status code.
exercises.instructions:
What HTTP status code should each operation return on SUCCESS?

```
GET /api/products         → found 5 products
GET /api/products/P001   → product found
GET /api/products/P999   → product not found
POST /api/products        → product created
PUT /api/products/P001   → product updated
DELETE /api/products/P001 → product deleted
POST /api/products/P001/duplicate → P001 already exists
```
exercises.expected_solution:
GET all → 200
GET found → 200
GET not found → 404
POST create → 201
PUT update → 200
DELETE → 204
POST duplicate → 409

test_cases.expected:
GET all → 200
GET found → 200
GET not found → 404
POST create → 201
PUT update → 200
DELETE → 204
POST duplicate → 409

hints: 201 = Created (only for POST that creates)
204 = No Content (DELETE success — nothing to return)
409 = Conflict (duplicate resource)

exercises.title: Search endpoint with multiple query params
exercises.scenario: Product search API with category, price range, and pagination.
exercises.instructions:
Write `ProductController.search()` handling:
`GET /api/products/search?category=Electronics&minPrice=500&maxPrice=5000&page=0&size=10`

All params optional with defaults. Return List<String> of matching descriptions.
hints: required = false for fully optional params
defaultValue must be a String even for numeric params
All params can be absent — method must work with all defaults

exercises.title: @WebMvcTest — test the controller layer
exercises.scenario: Test HTTP behavior without starting a full server.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @WebMvcTest for `OrderController.getById()` that:
1. Tests 200 OK when order found (mock service)
2. Tests 404 when order not found
3. Verifies JSON response body contains orderId

Use MockMvc with @MockBean for OrderService.
hints: @WebMvcTest: loads only the web layer, not full context
@MockBean: Mockito mock registered in Spring context
jsonPath('$.id'): asserts on JSON field
status().isOk() = 200; status().isNotFound() = 404

exercises.title: Full CRUD controller with @WebMvcTest
exercises.scenario: Build and test a full CRUD controller.
exercises.instructions:
Build a `BookController` with full CRUD for books (title, author, isbn), AND write @WebMvcTest for:
1. GET all → 200 + JSON array
2. POST create → 201 + book with id
3. GET not found → 404
4. DELETE existing → 204

Includes request/response records, MockMvc tests.
hints: MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON for POST content type
jsonPath('$[0].title') for first array element
@MockBean registers mock in Spring context for @WebMvcTest

exercises.title: Flipkart-style product search API
exercises.scenario: Real-world search endpoint with filtering and pagination.
exercises.instructions:
Write `ProductSearchController.search()` with:
- GET /api/v1/products/search
- Params: q (query, required), category (optional), minPrice/maxPrice (optional, defaults 0/999999), sort (optional, 'price' or 'name'), page (default 0), size (default 24)
- Returns SearchResponse(List<ProductSummary> items, int total, int page, int size)

Also write @WebMvcTest for: search with q='mouse' → 200 with items, search without q → 400.
hints: q is required (no defaultValue, no required=false)
SearchResponse wraps items + pagination metadata
400 Bad Request for blank q: ResponseEntity.badRequest().build()

exercises.title: Complete REST API with full test suite
exercises.instructions:
Build a Razorpay-style `PaymentController` with:
- POST /api/payments → create payment (201)
- GET /api/payments/{id} → get by id (200 or 404)
- GET /api/payments?userId={id}&status={s} → filter (200)
- PUT /api/payments/{id}/capture → capture payment (200 or 404)

Write 6 @WebMvcTest tests:
1. Create → 201 + id in body
2. Get found → 200 + correct status field
3. Get not found → 404
4. Filter by userId → 200 + correct count
5. Capture payment → 200
6. Capture non-existent → 404
hints: PUT /payments/{id}/capture: path is /{id}/capture
jsonPath('$.length()') counts array elements
@MockBean in @WebMvcTest: Spring test replaces the real service with a mock
